title: Melody Incipit
description: Canonical data model for the Melody Incipit Grid Tool. Lanes are stored as per-subdivision cell arrays; aligned text, Markdown, and plain-text outputs are renderings of this structure, never the source of truth.

id.description: Stable identifier, normally date + slug fragment.

song_id.description: Connects the incipit to a larger song project.

audio_ref.description: Voice memo, Logic file, or recording link (free text in v1).

mode.description: Mode / scale collection. Suggested vocabulary: major, natural minor, harmonic minor, melodic minor, major pentatonic, minor pentatonic, blues, mixolydian, dorian, lydian, phrygian, chromatic color, uncertain.

meter.description: Document default meter. Fully supported: 4/4, 3/4, 2/4, 6/8. Other values are accepted and rendered with a 4/4-style grid plus a notice.

feel.description: Suggested vocabulary: straight, swing, shuffle, gospel sway, 6/8 ballad, funk, folk, hymnlike, cinematic, rubato, spoken-like.

hook_function.description: Suggested vocabulary: opening line, chorus hook, verse motif, call-and-response, tag ending, melodic answer, riff, vocal run, countermelody.

status.description: Suggested vocabulary: raw, promising, hook, verse, chorus, bridge, motif, developed, archived.

bar.description: degrees and lyrics are parallel arrays whose length equals the subdivision count implied by meter x grid_unit. Empty string = rest/sustain space; "-" = explicit hold. Accidentals are stored ASCII (b3, #4) and rendered as flat/sharp glyphs by the display layer.

chords.description: v1 always contains exactly one chord at position 0. The array form keeps chord-per-beat possible later without a schema break.
